title: Red
description: The transition era with emotional depth

songs.title: State of Grace
songs.lyrics:
I'm walking fast through the traffic lights
Busy streets and busy lives
And all we know is touch and go
We are alone with our changing minds
We fall in love 'til it hurts or bleeds
Or fades in time

And I never (Never)
Saw you coming
And I’ll never (Never)
Be the same

You come around and the armor falls
Pierce the room like a cannonball
Now, all we know is don’t let go
We are alone, just you and me
Up in your room and our slates are clean
Just twin fire signs, four blue eyes

So you were never a saint
And I've loved in shades of wrong
We learn to live with the pain
Mosaic broken hearts
But this love is brave and wild

And I never (Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh) (Never)
Saw you coming (Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh, Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh)
And I’ll never (Never) (Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh, Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh)
Be the same  (Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh, Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh)

This is a state of grace
This is the worthwhile fight
Love is a ruthless game
Unless you play it good and right
These are the hands of fate
You’re my Achilles heel
This is the golden age
Of something good and right and real

And I never (Never)
Saw you coming
And I’ll never
Be the same
And I never (Never)  (Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh, Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh)
Saw you coming (Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh, Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh)
And I’ll never (So you were never a saint)
(And I've loved in shades of wrong) (Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh, Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh)
Be the same (We learn to live with the pain) (Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh, Oh oh, oh oh oh-oh-oh)
(Mosaic broken hearts)
(But this love is brave and wild)

This is a state of grace
This is the worthwhile fight
Love is a ruthless game
Unless you play it good and right

vocab.definition: a large artillery shell that explodes on impact, often used metaphorically to describe a powerful or overwhelming force
vocab.lyric_snippet: You come around and the armor falls Pierce the room like a cannonball
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe the impact of the person's presence on her life, implying a sudden and powerful change.

vocab.definition: a person who is considered to be morally perfect or without fault, often used to describe someone who is perceived as being flawless or ideal
vocab.lyric_snippet: So you were never a saint
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe the person she's singing about, implying that they're not perfect and have flaws.

vocab.definition: a picture or image made from small pieces of material, such as tiles or glass, often used to describe something that is composed of many different parts
vocab.lyric_snippet: Mosaic broken hearts
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe the broken hearts of herself and the person she's singing about, implying that their love is a complex and multifaceted thing.

vocab.definition: the inevitable course of events that will happen to a person or thing, often used to describe something that is predetermined or unavoidable
vocab.lyric_snippet: These are the hands of fate
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe the idea that their love is destined to happen, implying that it's something that was meant to be.

vocab.definition: a legendary hero in Greek mythology who was invincible except for his heel, often used to describe someone who has a weakness or vulnerability
vocab.lyric_snippet: You’re my Achilles heel
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe the person she's singing about, implying that they're her weakness or vulnerability.

vocab.definition: cruel or merciless, often used to describe something that is brutal or unyielding
vocab.lyric_snippet: Love is a ruthless game
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe the idea that love can be cruel or merciless, implying that it's a challenging and unforgiving thing.

vocab.definition: something that is considered to be of high quality or value, often used to describe something that is desirable or attractive
vocab.lyric_snippet: This is the golden age
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word to describe the idea that their love is in a special or desirable state, implying that it's something to be cherished or valued.

songs.title: Red
songs.lyrics:
Loving him is like driving a new Maserati down a dead-end street
Faster than the wind, passionate as sin, ending so suddenly
Loving him is like trying to change your mind once you're already flying through the free fall
Like the colors in autumn, so bright just before they lose it all

Losing him was blue like I'd never known
Missing him was dark gray, all alone
Forgetting him was like trying to know somebody you've never met
But loving him was red

(Red, red)
(Red, red)
Loving him was red
(Red, red)
(Red, red)

Touching him was like realizing all you ever wanted was right there in front of you
Memorizing him was as easy as knowing all the words to your old favorite song
Fighting with him was like trying to solve a crossword and realizing there's no right answer
Regretting him was like wishing you never found out that love could be that strong

Losing him was blue like I'd never known
Missing him was dark gray, all alone (Whoa)
Forgetting him was like trying to know somebody you've never met
But loving him was red

(Red, red)
Oh, red (Red, red)
Burning red (Red, red)
(Red, red)

Remembering him comes in flashbacks and echoes
Tell myself it's time now, gotta let go
But moving on from him is impossible
When I still see it all in my head
In burning red
Burning, it was red

Oh, losing him was blue like I'd never known
Missing him was dark gray, all alone (Whoa)
Forgetting him was like trying to know somebody you've never met
'Cause loving him was red

(Red, red) Yeah, yeah, red (Red, red)
Burning red (Red, red)
(Red, red)

And that's why (Red, red) he's spinning 'round in my head
Comes back to me (Red, red), burning red
(Red, red) Yeah, yeah
(Red, red)

His love was like driving a new Maserati down a dead-end street

vocab.definition: having or showing a strong and barely controllable emotion, especially desire or enthusiasm
vocab.lyric_snippet: Faster than the wind, passionate as sin
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the intensity of her feelings for the person she loves, emphasizing the overwhelming and uncontrollable nature of her passion.

vocab.definition: the season of the year between summer and winter, typically characterized by falling leaves and cooler temperatures
vocab.lyric_snippet: Like the colors in autumn, so bright just before they lose it all
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the vibrant colors of the person she loves, which are fleeting and will eventually fade.

vocab.definition: to learn or remember something, especially by repetition or practice
vocab.lyric_snippet: Memorizing him was as easy as knowing all the words to your old favorite song
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe how easily she can recall memories of the person she loves, making it seem effortless and natural.

vocab.definition: a puzzle consisting of a grid of squares, some of which contain words or phrases to be filled in
vocab.lyric_snippet: Fighting with him was like trying to solve a crossword and realizing there's no right answer
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the frustrating and futile nature of trying to resolve conflicts with the person she loves.

vocab.definition: a faint or repeated sound, especially one that is heard after the original sound has stopped
vocab.lyric_snippet: Remembering him comes in flashbacks and echoes
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the lingering memories and emotions that continue to haunt her after the person she loves is gone.

vocab.definition: not able to be done or achieved
vocab.lyric_snippet: But moving on from him is impossible
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the overwhelming difficulty of letting go of the person she loves and moving on with her life.

vocab.definition: lasting for a very short time
vocab.lyric_snippet: Like the colors in autumn, so bright just before they lose it all
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the transitory nature of the person she loves, emphasizing how quickly their presence can fade.

songs.title: Treacherous
songs.lyrics:
Put your lips close to mine
As long as they don't touch
Out of focus, eye to eye
'Til the gravity's too much
And I'll do anything you say
If you say it with your hands
And I'd be smart to walk away
But you're quicksand

This slope is treacherous
This path is reckless
This slope is treacherous
And I-I-I like it

I can't decide if it's a choice
Getting swept away
I hear the sound of my own voice
Asking you to stay
And all we are is skin and bone
Trained to get along
Forever going with the flow
But you're friction

This slope is treacherous
This path is reckless
This slope is treacherous
I, I, I like it

Two headlights shine through the sleepless night
And I will get you, get you alone
Your name has echoed through my mind
And I just think you should, think you should know
That nothing safe is worth the drive
And I would follow you, follow you home
I'll follow you, follow you home

This hope is treacherous
This daydream is dangerous
This hope is treacherous
I-I-I, I-I-I, I-I-I

Two headlights shine through the sleepless night
And I will get you, get you alone
Your name has echoed through my mind
And I just think you should, think you should know
That nothing safe is worth the drive
And I would follow you, follow you home
I'll follow you, follow you home
I'll follow you, follow you home
I'll follow you, follow you home

This slope is treacherous
I-I-I like it

vocab.definition: a mixture of water and sand that can quickly suck in and trap objects or people
vocab.lyric_snippet: But you're quicksand
vocab.context: Taylor uses quicksand to describe the person she's drawn to, implying they're unpredictable and potentially hazardous.

vocab.definition: a force that opposes motion between two surfaces that are in contact
vocab.lyric_snippet: But you're friction
vocab.context: Taylor uses friction to describe the person she's drawn to, implying they create resistance and tension in her life.

vocab.definition: showing a lack of concern for the consequences of one's actions
vocab.lyric_snippet: This path is reckless
vocab.context: Taylor uses reckless to describe the path she's on, implying she's taking risks and disregarding potential consequences.

vocab.definition: hazardous or difficult to navigate
vocab.lyric_snippet: This slope is treacherous
vocab.context: Taylor uses treacherous to describe the situation she's in, implying it's hazardous and potentially threatening.

vocab.definition: carried away by a strong force, such as a current or a crowd
vocab.lyric_snippet: Getting swept away
vocab.context: Taylor uses swept to describe her feelings, implying she's being carried away by her emotions and losing control.

vocab.definition: repeated or reflected back, often faintly
vocab.lyric_snippet: Your name has echoed through my mind
vocab.context: Taylor uses echoed to describe the persistence of her thoughts, implying her mind is preoccupied with the person she's thinking of.

vocab.definition: without sleep; unable to sleep
vocab.lyric_snippet: Two headlights shine through the sleepless night
vocab.context: Taylor uses sleepless to describe the night, implying it's dark and devoid of rest.

songs.title: I Knew You Were Trouble

Once upon a time, a few mistakes ago
I was in your sights, you got me alone
You found me, you found me
You found me-e-e-e-e
I guess you didn't care and I guess I liked that
And when I fell hard, you took a step back
Without me, without me
Without me-e-e-e-e

And he's long gone when he's next to me
And I realize the blame is on me

'Cause I knew you were trouble when you walked in
So shame on me now
Flew me to places I'd never been
'Til you put me down, oh
I knew you were trouble when you walked in
So shame on me now
Flew me to places I'd never been
Now, I'm lying on the cold, hard ground

Oh, oh-oh
Trouble, trouble, trouble
Oh, oh-oh
Trouble, trouble, trouble

No apologies, he'll never see you cry
Pretends he doesn't know that he's the reason why
You're drowning, you're drowning
You're drowning-ing-ing-ing-ing
And I heard you moved on from whispers on the street
A new notch in your belt is all I'll ever be
And now, I see, now, I see
Now, I see-e-e-e-e

He was long gone when he met me
And I realize the joke is on me, yeah

I knew you were trouble when you walked in (Oh)
So shame on me now
Flew me to places I'd never been
'Til you put me down, oh
I knew you were trouble when you walked in
So shame on me now
Flew me to places I'd never been (Yeah)
Now, I'm lying on the cold, hard ground

Oh, oh-oh
Trouble, trouble, trouble (Yeah, trouble)
Oh, oh-oh
Trouble, trouble, trouble

And the saddest fear
Comes creeping in
That you never loved me
Or her, or anyone, or anything, yeah

I knew you were trouble when you walked in
So shame on me now
Flew me to places I'd never been (Never been)
'Til you put me down, oh
I knew you were trouble when you walked in (Knew it right there)
So shame on me now (Knew it right there)
Flew me to places I'd never been (Ooh)
Now, I'm lying on the cold, hard ground

Oh, oh-oh
Trouble, trouble, trouble (Oh)
Oh, oh-oh
Trouble, trouble, trouble
I knew you were trouble when you walked in
Trouble, trouble, trouble
I knew you were trouble when you walked in
Trouble, trouble, trouble

vocab.definition: the act of seeing or perceiving something
vocab.lyric_snippet: I was in your sights, you got me alone
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe being in the other person's line of sight, implying a sense of vulnerability.

vocab.definition: errors or blunders that have been made
vocab.lyric_snippet: Once upon a time, a few mistakes ago
vocab.context: Taylor references past mistakes as a way of acknowledging her own role in the relationship's downfall.

vocab.definition: a small mark or indentation, especially one made in a surface
vocab.lyric_snippet: A new notch in your belt is all I'll ever be
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe being a mere conquest or trophy for the other person.

vocab.definition: soft, quiet talk or rumors
vocab.lyric_snippet: A new notch in your belt is all I'll ever be / And now, I heard you moved on from whispers on the street
vocab.context: Taylor references rumors or gossip about the other person's infidelity.

vocab.definition: a situation or circumstance that is humorous or ironic
vocab.lyric_snippet: He was long gone when he met me / And I realize the joke is on me, yeah
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the irony of being in a relationship with someone who was already over her.

vocab.definition: moving slowly and quietly, often in a stealthy or sinister way
vocab.lyric_snippet: And the saddest fear / Comes creeping in
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the fear of being unwanted or unloved, which is slowly creeping into her mind.

songs.title: All Too Well
songs.lyrics:
I walked through the door with you, the air was cold
But something 'bout it felt like home somehow
And I left my scarf there at your sister's house
And you've still got it in your drawer, even now

Oh, your sweet disposition and my wide-eyed gaze
We're singing in the car, getting lost upstate
Autumn leaves falling down like pieces into place
And I can picture it after all these days

And I know it's long gone and
That magic's not here no more
And I might be okay, but I'm not fine at all
Oh, oh, oh

'Cause there we are again on that little town street
You almost ran the red 'cause you were lookin' over at me
Wind in my hair, I was there
I remember it all too well

Photo album on the counter, your cheeks were turning red
You used to be a little kid with glasses in a twin-sized bed
And your mother's telling stories 'bout you on the tee-ball team
You taught me 'bout your past, thinking your future was me

And I know it's long gone and
There was nothing else I could do
And I forget about you long enough
To forget why I needed to

'Cause there we are again in the middle of the night
We're dancing ‘round the kitchen in the refrigerator light
Down the stairs, I was there
I remember it all too well, yeah

Well, maybe we got lost in translation
Maybe I asked for too much
But maybe this thing was a masterpiece
'Til you tore it all up
Running scared, I was there
I remember it all too well
And you call me up again
Just to break me like a promise
So casually cruel in the name of being honest
I'm a crumpled up piece of paper lying here
'Cause I remember it all, all, all
Too well

Time won't fly, it's like I'm paralyzed by it
I'd like to be my old self again, but I'm still trying to find it
After plaid shirt days and nights when you made me your own
Now, you mail back my things and I walk home alone
But you keep my old scarf from that very first week
'Cause it reminds you of innocence and it smells like me
You can't get rid of it
'Cause you remember it all too well, yeah

'Cause there we are again when I loved you so
Back before you lost the one real thing you've ever known
It was rare, I was there
I remember it all too well

Wind in my hair, you were there
You remember it all
Down the stairs, you were there
You remember it all
It was rare, I was there
I remember it all too well

vocab.definition: A person's natural or habitual way of behaving or feeling.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Oh, your sweet disposition and my wide-eyed gaze
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'disposition' to describe the person's kind and gentle nature.

vocab.definition: Completely unable to move or act because of fear or shock.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Time won't fly, it's like I'm paralyzed by it
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'paralyzed' to describe her emotional state, feeling frozen in time.

vocab.definition: In a relaxed and informal way, without much thought or concern.
vocab.lyric_snippet: So casually cruel in the name of being honest
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'casually' to describe the person's behavior, implying a lack of empathy.

vocab.definition: Folded or twisted into a small, messy shape.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm a crumpled up piece of paper lying here
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'crumpled' to describe her emotional state, feeling broken and fragile.

vocab.definition: A state of being free from guilt, shame, or moral wrongdoing.
vocab.lyric_snippet: It reminds you of innocence and it smells like me
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'innocence' to describe a lost time in the relationship, before things became complicated.

vocab.definition: Happening or done only occasionally or in small numbers.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Back before you lost the one real thing you've ever known / It was rare
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'rare' to describe the quality of the relationship, implying it was special and unique.

vocab.definition: The act of changing words or ideas from one language to another.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Well, maybe we got lost in translation
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'translation' to describe a communication breakdown in the relationship.

vocab.definition: a subculture that values nonconformity and individuality, often characterized by a distinctive style or aesthetic
vocab.lyric_snippet: to dress up like hipsters
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'hipsters' to describe a style or aesthetic that she and her friends are adopting, likely as a way to poke fun at themselves and their youthful attempts at nonconformity

vocab.definition: feeling or causing great unhappiness or discomfort
vocab.lyric_snippet: It's miserable and magical
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'miserable' to describe the bittersweet feeling of being young and carefree, acknowledging that this stage of life can be both exhilarating and painful

vocab.definition: having a quality of enchantment or wonder
vocab.lyric_snippet: It's miserable and magical
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'magical' to describe the sense of possibility and enchantment that comes with being young and carefree

vocab.definition: a specific date or time by which something must be done
vocab.lyric_snippet: Tonight's the night when we forget about the deadlines
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'deadlines' to describe the responsibilities and pressures of adulthood, which she and her friends are temporarily escaping

vocab.definition: having mixed or unclear thoughts or feelings
vocab.lyric_snippet: We're happy, free, confused and lonely at the same time
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'confused' to describe the complex emotions and thoughts that come with being young and navigating relationships

vocab.definition: a painful or distressing experience, especially one caused by a romantic relationship
vocab.lyric_snippet: Tonight's the night when we forget about the heartbreaks
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'heartbreaks' to describe the painful experiences of past relationships, which she and her friends are temporarily forgetting

vocab.definition: to give a false or misleading impression
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'll bet you want to
vocab.context: Taylor uses the word 'betrays' to describe the way she thinks her friends might be giving a false impression of themselves, likely as a way to poke fun at the idea of trying to present a perfect image

songs.title: I Almost Do
songs.lyrics:
I bet this time of night, you’re still up
I bet you’re tired from a long, hard week

Sometimes, you wonder 'bout me

And I just wanna tell you
It takes everything in me not to call you
And I wish I could run to you
And I hope you know that
Every time I don’t, I almost do, I almost do

I bet you think I either moved on or hate you

That I can’t say hello to you and risk another goodbye

And I just wanna tell you
It takes everything in me not to call you
And I wish I could run to you
And I hope you know that
Every time I don’t, I almost do, I almost do

Oh, we made quite a mess, babe
It’s probably better off this way
And I confess, babe
In my dreams, you’re touching my face
And asking me if I want to try again with you
And I almost do

And I just wanna tell you
It takes everything in me not to call you
And I wish I could run to you
And I hope you know that
Every time I don’t, I almost do, I almost do

I bet this time of night, you’re still up
I bet you’re tired from a long, hard week
I bet you’re sittin' in your chair by the window
Looking out at the city, and I hope
Sometimes, you wonder 'bout me

vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to convey the idea that the listener never considered the speaker's feelings or actions.

vocab.definition: to admit or acknowledge something, often in a sincere or humble way

vocab.definition: a confusing or difficult situation

vocab.definition: genuine or true in one's words or actions
vocab.lyric_snippet: And I confess, babe

vocab.definition: the state of being open to attack or harm, often used to describe emotional openness
vocab.lyric_snippet: And I confess, babe
vocab.context: Taylor's use of 'confess' conveys vulnerability in her dreams about the past relationship.

songs.title: We Are Never Ever Getting Back Together

Then you come around again and say

songs.title: Holy Ground

Was holy ground

But I don't wanna dance
If I'm not dancing with you

And right there where we stood

vocab.definition: a section of a street or road
vocab.lyric_snippet: And we walked down the block to my car
vocab.context: Taylor uses a casual, everyday term to describe a familiar location.
